commands.review_summary:
The branch wraps Codex diagnostic tool name, description, and schema reads in safe accessors and adds regression coverage for throwing schema getters and hostile thrown values.

PR surface: Source +35, Tests +58. Total +93 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, though I did not execute the failing path: current main reads tool.inputSchema during diagnostic emitter construction, so a throwing getter aborts capture. The PR adds a focused regression test for that path.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the remaining action is normal maintainer draft, review, and validation completion.

target.title: fix(codex): harden diagnostic tool capture
target.body:
## Summary

- keep Codex model-call diagnostic tool-definition capture from throwing on unreadable tool schema/name/description accessors
- preserve readable tool definitions while replacing unreadable schemas with a small serializable diagnostic placeholder
- add regression coverage for throwing schema getters and hostile thrown values

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-diagnostics.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-diagnostics.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-diagnostics.test.ts`
- `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`
- direct `node --import tsx` repro: constructing `createCodexModelCallDiagnosticEmitter` with a throwing `inputSchema` getter now prints `ok`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: optional Codex diagnostic capture can no longer abort attempt startup while trying to record malformed tool definitions.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw Codex extension test lane via repo wrapper; direct `tsx` diagnostic-emitter construction repro.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/attempt-diagnostics.test.ts --reporter=dot`; direct `node --import tsx` repro invoking `createCodexModelCallDiagnosticEmitter`; Blacksmith/Testbox availability check.

Evidence after fix: focused test passed, 1 file / 2 tests; direct repro printed `ok`; oxlint and diff check passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix: diagnostic tool-definition capture returns serializable fallback schema data for the unreadable tool and continues instead of throwing.

What was not tested: remote `pnpm check:changed` did not run. Blacksmith Testbox is not authenticated in this shell. AWS Crabbox was not retried for this slice because the previous wrapper run failed locally creating its temporary full checkout with `No space left on device`; disk remains critically low.
target.author: vincentkoc
